Home / Naturopathy

Online Naturopathy Consultation

Work with a practitioner in naturopathic disciplines (Italian Law 4/2013): explore mindful eating, traditional medicinal herbs, and daily wellbeing rhythms. In Italy, naturopathy is not a healthcare profession — it sits alongside your medical care, it does not replace it.

Book a Consultation Learn More
Understanding Naturopathy

What is Naturopathy?

Naturopathy is a holistic approach to wellness that works with the body's innate intelligence to restore balance and support overall vitality. Rather than treating isolated symptoms, naturopathy looks at the whole person — examining diet, digestion, sleep, stress, environment, and emotional state — to identify the underlying factors contributing to imbalance. This comprehensive perspective allows practitioners to address root causes rather than simply managing surface-level symptoms.

The roots of naturopathic medicine lie in the Hippocratic tradition (the natural power of nature — or in naturopathic terms, Vis Medicatrix Naturae), combined with modern nutritional science, botanical medicine, and functional approaches to health. A qualified naturopath draws on a range of tools including nutritional therapy, herbal medicine, lifestyle medicine, and other natural modalities to create a personalised protocol tailored to the individual.

Online naturopathy consultations make this kind of comprehensive, individualised care accessible from anywhere. Whether you're looking to address a specific concern — digestive issues, fatigue, hormonal imbalance, skin conditions — or simply to optimise your everyday wellbeing, a naturopath can offer a thorough, personalised roadmap to greater vitality and balance.

The Process

How a Naturopathy Session Works

01

Detailed Intake

Complete a comprehensive questionnaire on your health history, diet, lifestyle, energy patterns, and wellness goals.

02

Consultation

A live session to explore your daily habits, diet, sleep-wake rhythms, perceived energy levels, and wellbeing goals together.

03

Assessment

Together, the practitioner identifies the lifestyle areas to work on over the coming weeks.

04

Personalised Protocol

Written suggestions on mindful eating, traditionally used medicinal herbs, and any over-the-counter supplements, along with lifestyle adjustments. These are not medical prescriptions or personalised diets: for those, consult a doctor, qualified nutritional biologist, or registered dietitian.

Key Benefits

Why Choose Naturopathy

🌿

Whole-Body Approach

Naturopathy takes an integrated approach to lifestyle, diet, and daily rhythms — it accompanies your medical care as a bio-natural practice, it does not replace it.

🥗

Personalised Nutrition

Receive a dietary plan built specifically for your body, goals, and lifestyle — not generic advice.

🌱

Approccio bionaturale

From herbal tinctures to targeted supplements and lifestyle practices, naturopaths work with nature's full pharmacy.

Frequently Asked Questions

A practitioner in naturopathic disciplines explores your lifestyle, diet, daily rhythms, and habits with you. They suggest good practices, mindful eating, and traditional medicinal herbs. In Italy, naturopathy falls among the non-regulated professions (Law 4/2013) and is a bio-natural practice, not a medical act.

Yes. Online consultations are highly effective for naturopathy, as most of the assessment is based on your history, symptoms, and lifestyle — all of which can be thoroughly explored via video call.

Naturopaths work with a wide range of concerns including digestive health, fatigue, hormonal balance, skin conditions, stress, immunity, and general vitality optimisation.

No. In Italy, naturopathy is not a healthcare profession: it falls among the non-regulated professions (Law 4/2013) and is classified as a bio-natural practice. Some elements (mindful eating, traditional herbal medicine) have mixed scientific research — the evidence is not conclusive and should always be combined with your doctor's advice.

Naturopathy focuses on identifying the root causes of imbalance and supporting the body's natural vitality, rather than primarily managing symptoms with pharmaceuticals. It is complementary to, not a replacement for, conventional medical care.
